macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh confirmed as next Federal Reserve Chair, replacing Powell — viewed as more hawkish on inflation, skeptical of QE, and historically critical of large Fed balance sheet.
primary_drivers: Hawkish shift in monetary policy reaction function — higher-for-longer rates expectation
Steeper yield curve / higher long-end yields as QE/balance sheet expansion becomes less likely
Stronger USD on relative hawkishness vs other G10 central banks

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US money-center bank with significant asset-sensitive NIM exposure; steeper curve and higher-for-longer short rates expand net interest margin. Warsh has publicly favored normalized rates which directly benefit bank NII.
Most asset-sensitive of the big banks — every 100bp parallel shift adds ~$3B to NII per company disclosures. Hawkish Fed chair = higher front-end rates held longer.
Charles Schwab earnings highly levered to short rates via client cash sweep spreads; hawkish Warsh reduces risk of aggressive cuts that compressed 2024-25 earnings.
Life insurer with long-duration liabilities; higher long-end yields from less QE expectation improve reinvestment yields on fixed income portfolio and spread business.

losers.rationale: Representative high-multiple, long-duration growth name trading on forward FCF discounting; extremely sensitive to terminal rate assumptions. Warsh hawkishness pressures the multiple.
Highly levered consumer-credit-dependent business — higher-for-longer rates raise its own financing costs and squeeze subprime auto buyer affordability simultaneously.

headlines: Kevin Warsh wins Senate confirmation as the next Federal Reserve chair

macro_events.event_summary: Mortgage rates have spiked to their highest level since March following hotter-than-expected inflation data, pressuring housing affordability and rate-sensitive sectors.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end yields raising 30-yr mortgage rates and crushing affordability
Sticky inflation pushing back Fed cut expectations / higher-for-longer
Reduced mortgage origination and refi volume

beneficiaries.rationale: Owns Ellie Mae/Black Knight mortgage tech stack — benefits relatively from servicing economics as MSR values rise with higher rates (servicing portfolios become more valuable when prepay speeds slow). Net positive despite lower origination volume.
Apartment REIT benefits from would-be homebuyers being priced out of purchase market and forced into rental demand; pricing power on renewals improves as for-sale market freezes.
Sunbelt apartment REIT in markets (Atlanta, Dallas, Raleigh) where the rent-vs-buy gap widens most dramatically when 30-yr mortgage rates spike — captures shadow demand from frustrated buyers.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder by volume and most exposed to entry-level buyer affordability; higher mortgage rates directly suppress order volume and force larger rate-buydown incentives that compress gross margins.
Lennar similarly entry-level skewed; will need to ramp incentives/buydowns to maintain absorption pace, hitting margins. Stock historically tracks 10Y yield inversely with high beta.
Rocket Companies is a pure-play mortgage originator with heavy refi mix exposure; higher rates collapse the refi pipeline that drives its highest-margin volume.
UWM Holdings is the largest wholesale mortgage lender — directly levered to origination volume which contracts sharply when 30-yr crosses key affordability thresholds.
Zillow revenue tied to housing transaction volume (Premier Agent, mortgage marketplace via Zillow Home Loans); transaction freeze on rate spike directly hits lead-gen monetization.
Compass is a pure-play residential brokerage with commission revenue directly tied to existing home sales volume, which collapses when rates spike and lock-in effect intensifies.
Builders FirstSource sells lumber/structural products to homebuilders; new construction starts decline with mortgage rate spikes, hitting single-family volume which is BLDR's core mix.

headlines: Mortgage rates jump to highest level since March on hotter inflation reports

macro_events.event_summary: Traders expect Trump's China visit to yield a tariff truce extension and large Boeing aircraft orders from Chinese carriers.
primary_drivers: Tariff truce extension reduces cost/margin pressure on China-exposed importers and exporters
Direct Boeing order announcements drive backlog and revenue visibility for BA and its supply chain
Improved US-China trade sentiment supports China ADRs and multinationals with significant China revenue

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct beneficiary of any announced Chinese aircraft purchase order — China has been largely absent from Boeing's order book since 2019; a deal directly adds to backlog and reopens a key growth market.
GE Aerospace supplies LEAP engines (via CFM JV) for the 737 MAX; Chinese Boeing orders directly translate to engine and aftermarket revenue.
Major Boeing supplier (avionics, APUs); benefits from incremental 737/787 deliveries to China.
Tariff truce extension reduces the risk of a renewed cross-Pacific freight slowdown; FedEx has direct China-US air freight exposure.
Largest US auto OEM with major China manufacturing and revenue exposure (Shanghai gigafactory); tariff truce reduces risk of retaliatory measures on US autos in China.
Significant China construction/mining revenue exposure; tariff truce extension reduces risk of Chinese procurement retaliation against US industrials.

headlines: Trump in China: Traders predict a tariff truce extension and Boeing aircraft purchases

macro_events.event_summary: 10-year Treasury yield hit a new YTD high after a hotter-than-expected PPI print, reinforcing sticky inflation and pushing the long end higher.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end yields raise discount rates on long-duration equities
Hot PPI signals producer-level inflation passthrough pressure on margins
Steeper/higher curve expands net interest margin for banks

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab's earnings are highly sensitive to short/long rates via cash sweep reinvestment yields and reduced unrealized AFS losses; higher 10Y directly improves NIM and book value trajectory.
Largest beneficiary among money-center banks of higher long-end yields via securities reinvestment and loan repricing; asset-sensitive balance sheet.
Life insurers earn spread on long-duration fixed-income portfolios; higher 10Y yields directly lift new-money reinvestment rates and statutory capital.
P&C insurer with large float invested in intermediate Treasuries; rising yields boost investment income, a key earnings driver in 2026.

losers.rationale: Realty Income — bond-proxy net-lease REIT with long-duration cash flows and rate-sensitive cap rates; trades inversely to 10Y yield.
Tower REIT carrying significant leverage with long-dated contractual revenue; valuation pressure from higher discount rate and refi cost.
Cash-burning EV maker reliant on capital markets; higher yields raise cost of future capital raises and compress terminal value in DCF.

headlines: 10-year Treasury yield hits new high for the year after very hot producer prices reading

macro_events.event_summary: MarketWatch analysis warns an Iran war could be a ~$300B economic shock, pushing mortgage rates higher and squeezing wages via oil price spike and inflation.
primary_drivers: Oil price spike from Middle East supply disruption / Strait of Hormuz risk
Higher long-end yields as inflation expectations rise, lifting mortgage rates

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; direct earnings leverage to a Brent spike from a Persian Gulf conflict, plus US production benefits from lost Iranian/Hormuz barrels.
Same crude-price leverage as XOM; US-domiciled production reduces sanctions/supply risk relative to peers with Middle East assets.
Highly oil-levered Permian pure-play with no Middle East assets; historically one of the highest-beta names to Brent shocks.
Direct beneficiary of higher defense outlays in a Middle East war scenario — missile defense (THAAD, PAC-3) and F-35 demand from Gulf allies and US replenishment.
Patriot/Stinger/Tomahawk manufacturer — these specific munitions get consumed and reordered in any Iran conflict; visible backlog catalyst.
B-21, ICBM, and missile defense exposure; geopolitical escalation supports multi-year budget visibility.
Texas Pacific Land — royalty interests on Permian acreage capture oil price upside with no opex; pure leverage to crude.
Cheniere benefits as European/Asian buyers pay up for US LNG if Gulf LNG (Qatar) shipping is threatened via Hormuz.

losers.rationale: Largest US homebuilder — directly hit by higher mortgage rates compressing affordability and cancellations; entry-level buyer most sensitive.
Same mortgage-rate transmission as DHI; high sensitivity to 30-year fixed rates which track the 10Y plus inflation premium.
Southwest has no fuel hedges in place historically and runs thin margins; jet fuel spike from oil shock hits earnings directly.
Jet fuel is ~25% of opex; even with hedges, sustained Brent>$100 erodes margins and international demand softens on travel-risk perception.
Cruise operator — bunker fuel costs surge and Mediterranean/Red Sea itineraries face cancellation risk in a Middle East war.
Low-income consumer base most squeezed by simultaneous gasoline and mortgage/rent pressure; trade-down helps but margin mix worsens.
Zillow — transaction volumes (Premier Agent revenue) fall when mortgage rates spike and housing turnover freezes.
Rocket Companies — mortgage originator with refi-heavy mix; volumes collapse on a rate spike, direct revenue hit.

headlines: The Iran war could be a $300 billion shock — driving up mortgage rates and squeezing wages
